name: Cheshire (United Kingdom)
description: A blue field bearing a yellow wheatsheaf between three yellow wheatsheaves and a sword in the lower hoist, with the field divided to suggest a sword across — based on the arms of the Earls of Chester.
history: Based on the arms of the historic Earldom of Chester; registered with the Flag Institute as the county flag of Cheshire on 7 June 2013.

features.symbolism: from the arms of the Earls of Chester

features.role: Sword of the Earl of Chester
features.symbolism: the ceremonial sword of the Earldom
